options.label: A
options.text: A turning effect produced by a force

options.label: B
options.text: The speed at which something moves

options.label: C
options.text: The amount of matter in an object

correct_answer: A turning effect produced by a force
explanation: A moment is the turning effect that occurs when a force is applied at a distance from a pivot point (like a fulcrum). The further away the force is applied from the pivot, the greater the turning effect. This is the fundamental concept behind levers and seesaws.
